senses.definition: to suddenly fall on the ground, often as a way of avoiding danger

examples.x: Suddenly another soldier hits the dirt beside me.
When they heard the tornado sirens, the residents hit the dirt in their basements to stay safe.
The car suddenly swerved into their lane, and they had to hit the dirt to avoid a collision.
As the shooting started, the soldiers immediately began hitting the dirt for cover.
When the thunderstorm hits, my dog gets scared and hits the dirt, seeking refuge under the bed.

senses.definition: an area that is dangerous or not allowed for people to use for a particular purpose

examples.x: The soldiers were armed to the teeth with rifles, grenades, and body armor as they approached the enemy camp.
He walked into the negotiation room armed to the teeth with facts, figures, and a compelling argument.
The security team was armed to the teeth to protect the VIP during the high-risk event.
In the online gaming tournament, he entered the final round armed to the teeth with powerful upgrades and strategies.
The cybersecurity system was armed to the teeth with firewalls and encryption, making it nearly impenetrable to cyberattacks.

senses.definition: to shoot bullets from a gun

examples.x: He threatened to bust a cap if anyone came near his property without permission.
He once busted a cap to protect his family from a potential threat.
Last year, the gang members busted a cap to assert their dominance in the neighborhood.
If the intruders don't leave, he will likely threaten to bust a cap to protect his property.
In the intense negotiation, they are willing to bust a cap and walk away from the deal if their demands aren't met.

senses.definition: said to an enemy before shooting them dead with a gun

examples.x: The gangster threatened to make his enemies "eat lead" if they didn't comply with his demands.
During the heated argument, he yelled at his opponent to "shut up or eat lead."
The detective in the film warned the criminal, "Surrender now, or you're going to eat hot lead.
The sharpshooter taunted his opponent, saying, "Come on out, and you'll eat my lead!"

senses.definition: someone or something that lacks enough defense or protection and is easy to be targeted

examples.x: Without air support, the stranded soldiers were like sitting ducks for the enemy's artillery.
Leaving your car unlocked in this neighborhood is like making it a sitting duck for theft.
During the storm, the small fishing boat became a sitting duck, unable to navigate the rough waters.
Her lack of cybersecurity measures made her computer a sitting duck for hackers.
When you're the only candidate opposing the incumbent with widespread support, you can feel like a sitting duck in the political arena.

senses.definition: in the position or state of readiness for answering any attack or criticism

examples.x: During the battle, the infantry was on the offensive, launching a series of coordinated attacks against enemy positions.
The air force was on the offensive, conducting a series of bombing runs to disrupt the enemy's supply lines.
In response to the enemy's incursion, the military shifted from a defensive stance to being on the offensive, launching a counteroffensive to reclaim lost territory.

senses.definition: used for referring to a recent war or conflict

examples.x: The book goes over the late unpleasantness of the last election, and the ructions it has caused across the country.
In their family history, they often hear stories about their ancestors' experiences during the late unpleasantness.
The local museum has an exhibit dedicated to the artifacts and memorabilia from the late unpleasantness.
He wrote a book about the late unpleasantness, exploring the personal stories of individuals caught up in the conflict.
The old veterans' club frequently gathers to reminisce about the late unpleasantness, sharing their wartime anecdotes.

senses.definition: to use a sword in order to murder or execute someone
to die while actively engaged in one's work, often in a heroic or dedicated manner

examples.x: Bill died with his boots on, caught by suction in a waste pipe.
When the fearless cowboy met his end in a showdown with outlaws, he died with his boots on, just as he had always hoped.
The dedicated detective solved crimes until his very last day on the force, fulfilling his desire to die with his boots on.
The adventurous pilot, who loved to fly, tragically died in a plane crash, living up to his aspiration to die with his boots on.
Despite his old age, the hardworking farmer continued tending to his fields and passed away doing what he loved, truly dying with his boots on.

senses.definition: to shoot someone in their head and kill them
to discharge a large number of bullets or explosives

examples.x: During the battle, the soldiers were ordered to let loose a hail of bullets upon the enemy positions.
The artillery unit prepared to let loose a relentless barrage of cannon fire on the opposing forces.
As night fell, the archers let loose a volley of arrows into the darkness, hoping to hit their unseen foes.
The warships at sea readied their cannons to let loose a devastating broadside on the enemy fleet.
The commander decided to let loose the cavalry in a swift charge to break through the enemy's defenses.

senses.definition: to fight one's enemies or attackers that pursue one

examples.x: A dog barks and stands at bay if he sees any one assault his master.
The small group of soldiers had to stand at bay, defending their position against a much larger enemy force.

senses.definition: to engage in a physical fight, typically using one's fists

examples.x: The two boxers entered the ring and were ready to duke it out for the championship title.
In the schoolyard, the two bullies decided to duke it out after a heated argument.
The bar brawl escalated quickly as patrons began to duke it out over a disagreement.
The action movie's climax features a thrilling scene where the hero and the villain duke it out in an epic fight.
When tensions flared on the ice during the hockey game, the players dropped their gloves and started to duke it out in a fistfight.
